title: Teams cannot efficiently convert vague tickets into actionable work items
problem_statement: Engineering tickets often start vague, leaving teams unable to convert them into actionable work without significant rework or clarification cycles.
affected_users: Engineering teams and product managers who author, triage, or act on engineering tickets.
observable_symptom: Vague tickets enter the engineering workflow without structure or clear acceptance criteria, causing teams to struggle converting them into actionable work.
acceptance_criteria: Given a vague ticket input, the system produces a structured spec containing at minimum a problem statement, affected users, and acceptance criteria.
Each generated acceptance criterion is binary and testable without subjective judgment.
The structured spec is produced without requiring manual reformatting by the requester.
out_of_scope: Automatic implementation or resolution of the ticket contents.
Integration with any specific ticketing system unless explicitly scoped.
assumptions: A shared definition of 'structured spec' and its required fields exists or will be defined before implementation.
The capability targets engineering tickets specifically, not support tickets or other request types.

open_questions.question: What surface or interface will this capability be exposed through (e.g., web UI, CLI, API)?
What is the expected data source for incoming vague tickets (e.g., Jira, GitHub Issues, free-form text input)?
Is authentication or access control required to use the spec generation capability?
Is there an existing spec schema or template that output must conform to, or does one need to be defined?

success_metrics: Structured specs generated from vague tickets are accepted by engineering teams without requiring rework or additional clarification in the majority of cases.
non_functional_constraints: Generated specs must be consistent and reproducible for equivalent inputs.
The capability must be usable by non-technical stakeholders without specialized training.
